sections.paragraph:
σύνδουλος, ου, ὁ [σύν, δοῦλος] fellow slave

—a. a pers. who, along with others, is someone’s property Mt 24:49.

—b. in extended sense: of royal officials understood to be in a relationship of total obedience to their ruler Mt 18:28f, 31, 33. Sim. in Christian parlance relating to status shared in view of association with God or Jesus Christ Col 1:7; 4:7; Rv 6:11; in related vein 19:10; 22:9.
